_x000D_
Comments: system-wide studies of information needs and information systems, including information requirements, usage, sharing, acquisition, processing, storage, dissemination, deletion. Includes information sharing, particularly in the CDM context, also encompassing information exchange and sharing with external stakeholders in ATM. Information quality issues are covered. Includes information  requirements for controllers and pilots (e.g. flight plan and surveillance data, air derived data, met data, airspace structure data, ASM data); communications requirements for controllers and pilots. Links with CDM - 'Collaborative Decision Making', IKM 'Information management & Knowledge management'._x000D_
_x000D_
4. Radio Frequency Studies: Frequency Allocation, Frequency Coverage, Interference, Modulation Class, Spectrum Utilisation, Propagation, ITU._x000D_
_x000D_
Comments: studies of the allocation and usage of radio frequencies. Covers the entire useful RF spectrum, allocation to different services, geographical allocation, geographical coverage, changes of use, current and future usage, interference problems etc. ITU issues. Propagation studies, modulation techniques, spectrum utilisation efficiency, path reliability and related studies. RF Licensing and charges issues._x000D_
_x000D_
5. Capacity, Safety and Economic Appraisal Studies: Performance indicators, Cost-benefit analysis, CBA, System capacity, Service capacity, Airspace capacity, Capacity utilisation, Safety, Internal efficiency, System effectiveness, Service Levels, QoS, Delays, TLS, Resilience, Security, Charges, Simulations, Business Case, Basic traffic forecast._x000D_
_x000D_
Comments: identification of capacity, safety and efficiency measures, cost-benefit analyses, performance indicators. Includes studies of capacity and delay, ATM efficiency, safety, resilience, security. Includes application of fast-time and real-time simulations to assess capacity, safety etc., also cost-benefit assessments in the preparation of business cases.

Air Traffic Management - DEFINITION:_x000D_
_x000D_
 _x000D_
_x000D_
It comprises all the air traffic management related technologies including: overall ATM and ATM automated support, airspace management, flow and capacity management, systems (communications, navigation, surveillance sensor, etc…), airport traffic and operations, meteorological and ATM R&D management._x000D_
_x000D_
 _x000D_
_x000D_
(Source: ACARE Area 7 and PAE own definition)_x000D_
_x000D_
  - SUBDOMAINS:_x000D_
_x000D_
 _x000D_
_x000D_
1-G-1. Overall ATM _x000D_
_x000D_
1-G-2. Airspace Management _x000D_
_x000D_
1-G-3. Flow and Capacity Management _x000D_
_x000D_
1-G-4. Communications and Systems Technology _x000D_
_x000D_
1-G-5. Navigation Systems _x000D_
_x000D_
1-G-6. Surveillance Sensor Systems _x000D_
_x000D_
1-G-7. ATM Automated Support _x000D_
_x000D_
1-G-8. Airport Traffic Management _x000D_
_x000D_
1-G-9. Airport Operations _x000D_
_x000D_
1-G-10. Airline Operations _x000D_
_x000D_
1-G-11. Meteorological _x000D_
_x000D_
1-G-12. R&D Management and Co-ordination

Airports - DEFINITION:_x000D_
_x000D_
 _x000D_
_x000D_
It comprises all the airport related technologies including: security equipment, crisis management, airport external safety and airport security._x000D_
_x000D_
 _x000D_
_x000D_
(Source: ACARE Area 8 and PAE own definition)_x000D_
_x000D_
  - SUBDOMAINS:_x000D_
_x000D_
 _x000D_
_x000D_
1-H-1. Security Equipment _x000D_
_x000D_
1-H-2. Crisis Management _x000D_
_x000D_
1-H-3. Airport External Safety _x000D_
_x000D_
1-H-4. Airport Security _x000D_
_x000D_

Airport Security - DEFINITION:_x000D_
_x000D_
 _x000D_
_x000D_
Airport security measures are the physical protection measures required in order to protect aircraft, airport staff, passengers and crew when on the ground. This includes blocking terrorist access to the aircraft when on the ground. Specific technologies are addressed in the Security Equipment area. Links also with SSS – ‘Surveillance Sensor Systems’. Cybersecurity issues affecting airport systems and subsystems are also included here._x000D_
_x000D_
 _x000D_
_x000D_
(Source: ACARE Domain 804 and PAE insertion about Cybersecurity)_x000D_
_x000D_
  - SUBDOMAINS:_x000D_
_x000D_
 _x000D_
_x000D_
1. Surveillance systems - sensors (e.g. CCTV, radar/RF, IR, visual)_x000D_
_x000D_
2. Surveillance systems - processing (e.g. image motion stabilisation, automatic recognition of faces/number plates, video tracking)_x000D_
_x000D_
3. Passenger profiling, co-ordinated with law enforcement and intelligence databases, to detect possible terrorists and other security risks. Passengers flagged as high-risk should be diverted to a screening process with more intensive human interaction and sophisticated detection equipment. Possible establishment of pool_x000D_
_x000D_
of pre-screened passengers who can be processed through airport security more quickly._x000D_
_x000D_
4. Passenger identification and verification (e.g. biometrics - voice recognition, face recognition, hand geometry, fingerprints, iris recognition)_x000D_
_x000D_
5. Development of systems for strict accountability for luggage loaded on aircraft (e.g. Positive Passenger Bag Matching - PPBM)_x000D_
_x000D_
6. Tracking of cargo and baggage within the airport (e.g. RFID tags)_x000D_
_x000D_
7. Physical protection measures to prevent unauthorised entry into secure areas._x000D_
_x000D_
8. Cybersecurity affecting airport systems and subsystems.

Airport External Safety - DEFINITION:_x000D_
_x000D_
 _x000D_
_x000D_
Quantitative probabilistic assessment of risk to the population in the vicinity of an airport. The risk is composed of the following three submodels: accident probability per movement, the accident location and the resulting lethality. The risk can be expressed in terms of individual risk and / or societal risk, meaning risk imposed to a group of a certain size._x000D_
_x000D_
 _x000D_
_x000D_
(Source: ACARE Domain 803)_x000D_
_x000D_
  - SUBDOMAINS:_x000D_
_x000D_
 _x000D_
_x000D_
1. Development of methods, (sub)models and software tools to assess risk to the population in the vicinity of airports_x000D_
_x000D_
2. Risk assessments using these tools_x000D_
_x000D_
3. Support to development of appropriate risk tolerability criteria_x000D_
_x000D_
4. Validation and evaluation of risk models and data_x000D_
_x000D_
5. Collection of input data: population, aircraft movements, aircraft routes, historical accident data, terrain data

Crisis management - DEFINITION:_x000D_
_x000D_
 _x000D_
_x000D_
It is necessary for the aviation security system to be able to respond appropriately to a wide range of different incidents. The effective management of such crises will require the integration of the efforts of all security systems and personnel involved._x000D_
_x000D_
 _x000D_
_x000D_
(Source: ACARE Domain 802)_x000D_
_x000D_
  - SUBDOMAINS:_x000D_
_x000D_
 _x000D_
_x000D_
1. Operational planning tools (including decision-support systems)_x000D_
_x000D_
2. Interactive computer-based systems to model the effects of major incidents, to allow staff the opportunity to practise and develop their responsibilities, to stress networks, and to establish weak points in the chain of aviation security in a safe environment

Security Equipment - DEFINITION:_x000D_
_x000D_
 _x000D_
_x000D_
Systems for the detection and neutralisation of threats posed by weapons and explosives. Includes the use of advanced technology to eliminate the ability of terrorists to conceal improvised explosive devices, weapons, and flammable gas or liquid explosives on aircraft. Achieved by processing passengers, crew, baggage and cargo through weapons and explosives detection machines: portals for people to walk through, portable machines for screeners to use, and bulk screening for cargo and baggage._x000D_
_x000D_
 _x000D_
_x000D_
(Source: ACARE Domain 801)_x000D_
_x000D_
  - SUBDOMAINS:_x000D_
_x000D_
 _x000D_
_x000D_
1. Secure communications links between key parts in the aviation security system_x000D_
_x000D_
2. Containerised/Palletised Inspection Systems_x000D_
_x000D_
3. Trace and Bulk Explosive Detection (e.g. thermal neutron analysis (TNA), CTX machines, canine searches)_x000D_
_x000D_
4. Weapon Detection (e.g. metal detectors, passive millimetre wave imaging, invasive ionising radiation (X-rays) )

Human Information Processing - DEFINITION:_x000D_
_x000D_
 _x000D_
_x000D_
Research into the ways in which humans gather and process information, converting data into knowledge, studies of human sensory, perceptual and cognitive processes._x000D_
_x000D_
 _x000D_
_x000D_
(Source: ACARE Domain 902)_x000D_
_x000D_
  - SUBDOMAINS:_x000D_
_x000D_
 _x000D_
_x000D_
1. Knowledge engineering_x000D_
_x000D_
2. Information gathering_x000D_
_x000D_
3. Information processing (aircrew, ATM, ground staff)_x000D_
_x000D_
4. Cognitive aspects of operator performance_x000D_
_x000D_
5. Human error and reliability studies

Human Factors - DEFINITION:_x000D_
_x000D_
 _x000D_
_x000D_
It comprises all the human factors influencing the diverse aviation related tasks, including: man-machine interface, human information processing and performance modelling & enhancement. Also includes human health related aspects (survivability, stress and protection) and humans and security. Selection and training are also in this area._x000D_
_x000D_
 _x000D_
_x000D_
(Source: ACARE Area 9 and PAE own definition)_x000D_
_x000D_
  - SUBDOMAINS:_x000D_
_x000D_
 _x000D_
_x000D_
1-I-1. Human Factors Integration, Man-machine Interface _x000D_
_x000D_
1-I-2. Human Information Processing _x000D_
_x000D_
1-I-3. Human Performance Modelling & Enhancement _x000D_
_x000D_
1-I-4. Selection & Training _x000D_
_x000D_
1-I-5. Human Survivability, Protection and Stress Effects _x000D_
_x000D_
1-I-6. Human Element in Security _x000D_
_x000D_

Human Performance specific_arealing & Enhancement - DEFINITION:_x000D_
_x000D_
 _x000D_
_x000D_
Measuring and modelling the effectiveness of people in the aviation system, both on the ground and in the air. Includes the ATM and aircrew (pilot, navigator, flight engineer) requirements for the role of the human in the systems (i.e. display, ergonomics) including airborne, cockpit and ground control systems._x000D_
_x000D_
 _x000D_
_x000D_
(Source: ACARE Domain 903)_x000D_
_x000D_
  - SUBDOMAINS:_x000D_
_x000D_
 _x000D_
_x000D_
1. Crew workload optimisation, error-tolerant systems_x000D_
_x000D_
2. Measurement and modelling of controller and pilot performance in the ATM system_x000D_
_x000D_
3. Measurement and modelling of aircrew performance_x000D_
_x000D_
4. Evaluation of human factors procedures_x000D_
_x000D_
5. Team working_x000D_
_x000D_
6. Self separation_x000D_
_x000D_
7. The human as a supervisor (both the pilot and/or the controller)_x000D_
_x000D_
8. Tasks sharing, task delegation_x000D_
_x000D_
9. Pilot less a/c_x000D_
_x000D_
10. Research and techniques to sense and monitor human performance, including physiological sensing and monitoring_x000D_
_x000D_
11. Studies of interventions (excluding training) that enhance individual physical and mental performance

Selection & Training - DEFINITION:_x000D_
_x000D_
 _x000D_
_x000D_
Covers the requirements for recruitment, selection, training, licensing, working practices and staff management of all personnel in the aviation system, both in the air and on the ground. Addresses aptitude, personal qualities, physical characteristics, manpower modelling and system complementing._x000D_
_x000D_
 _x000D_
_x000D_
(Source: ACARE Domain 904)_x000D_
_x000D_
  - SUBDOMAINS:_x000D_
_x000D_
 _x000D_
_x000D_
1. Recruitment and selection of aircrew (methods, facilities, evaluation)_x000D_
_x000D_
2. Recruitment and selection of ATM personnel (methods, facilities, evaluation)_x000D_
_x000D_
3. Recruitment and selection of ground crew (methods, facilities, evaluation)_x000D_
_x000D_
4. Training of aircrew (methods, facilities, evaluation)_x000D_
_x000D_
5. Training of ATM personnel (methods, facilities, evaluation)_x000D_
_x000D_
6. Training of ground crew (methods, facilities, evaluation)_x000D_
_x000D_
7. Staff management and working practices (shift work, roistering etc)_x000D_
_x000D_
8. Certification and licensing aspects_x000D_
_x000D_
9. Training needs analysis_x000D_
_x000D_
10. Management of skill acquisition and skill fade

Human Factors Integration, Man-machine Interface - DEFINITION:_x000D_
_x000D_
 _x000D_
_x000D_
The integration of separate strands of human factors (HF), and the interfaces between humans and technologies, to provide a holistic picture of the role, effectiveness and improvement of human personnel in the aviation systems._x000D_
_x000D_
 _x000D_
_x000D_
(Source: ACARE Domain 901)_x000D_
_x000D_
  - SUBDOMAINS:_x000D_
_x000D_
 _x000D_
_x000D_
1. Human-machine/computer interface (HMI, HCI, MMI) - design and evaluation_x000D_
_x000D_
2. Displays - Colour, 3D display, Virtual reality, Information presentation_x000D_
_x000D_
3. HF integration_x000D_
_x000D_
4. Development of methods, tools and processes to support the integration of people with complex systems_x000D_
_x000D_
5. Vision modelling and auditory communication studies_x000D_
_x000D_
6. Anthropometric studies

Human Element in Security - DEFINITION:_x000D_
_x000D_
 _x000D_
_x000D_
Optimisation of human performance and contributions through better operator selection, training, and performance monitoring . Relates especially to the various detection technologies, but all personnel in them aviation transport system should be addressed._x000D_
_x000D_
 _x000D_
_x000D_
(Source: ACARE Domain 906)_x000D_
_x000D_
  - SUBDOMAINS:_x000D_
_x000D_
 _x000D_
_x000D_
1. Pilot training for response to hijack incidents (computer- and simulator-based)_x000D_
_x000D_
2. Baggage and cargo screener selection_x000D_
_x000D_
3. Baggage and cargo screener training (e.g. Threat Image Projection, performance management)_x000D_
_x000D_
4. Baggage and cargo screener management (shift and duty time rosters, team leadership)_x000D_
_x000D_
5. Background security checks for personnel having access to secure areas

category_label: Human Survivability, Protection and Stress Effects
text:
Human Survivability, Protection and Stress Effects - DEFINITION:_x000D_
_x000D_
 _x000D_
_x000D_
Studies relating to the impact of stressors on human performance, behaviours and well-being. Includes impact of irregular duty cycles, sleep loss, physical and mental strain, prevention of musculo-skeletal injuries._x000D_
_x000D_
Research to understand issues covering the physical and mental health (including stress) aspects of personnel, both in the air and on the ground._x000D_
_x000D_
 _x000D_
_x000D_
(Source: ACARE Domain 905)_x000D_
_x000D_
  - SUBDOMAINS:_x000D_
_x000D_
 _x000D_
_x000D_
1. Health and safety/occupational health aspects for aircrew (methods, facilities, evaluation)_x000D_
_x000D_
2. Health and safety/occupational health aspects for ATM personnel (methods, facilities, evaluation)_x000D_
_x000D_
3. Health and safety/occupational health aspects for ground crew (methods, facilities, evaluation)_x000D_
_x000D_
4. Health and safety/occupational health aspects for passengers (methods, facilities, evaluation)_x000D_
_x000D_
5. Personnel response to blast and fire (injuries sustained, casualty rates etc)_x000D_
_x000D_
6. Personnel protective systems - aircrew_x000D_
_x000D_
7. Personnel protective systems - passengers_x000D_
_x000D_
8. Personnel protective systems - ground crew

Hybrid & Electric Power and Propulsion - DEFINITION:_x000D_
_x000D_
 _x000D_
_x000D_
It includes all the concepts of turboelectric, hybrid and pure electric power plants._x000D_
_x000D_
 _x000D_
_x000D_
(Source: PAE own elaboration)_x000D_
_x000D_

 _x000D_

_x000D_
 Turbo-electric power plants_x000D_
 Hybrid power plants_x000D_
_x000D_
_x000D_
Electric power plants

Breakthrough technologies - DEFINITION:_x000D_
_x000D_
 _x000D_
_x000D_
New air transport paradigms may evolve from speculative, exploratory research and concept studies._x000D_
_x000D_
 _x000D_
_x000D_
(Source: ACARE Domain 1003 except subdomains 4, 5 and 6 included into 1-K UAS technologies)_x000D_
_x000D_
  - SUBDOMAINS:_x000D_
_x000D_
 _x000D_
_x000D_
1. New materials_x000D_
_x000D_
2. Advanced design technologies_x000D_
_x000D_
3. Alternative fuels_x000D_
_x000D_
4. Technology watching_x000D_
_x000D_
5. Nanotechnology

category_label: Unconventional configurations and new aircraft concepts
text:
Unconventional configurations and new aircraft concepts - DEFINITION:_x000D_
_x000D_
 _x000D_
_x000D_
Radically new concepts in aircraft design could provide step changes - in flexibility of cabin layout (allowing for more specific tailoring of cabin space for business or leisure purposes) and reductions in operating costs resulting from highly integrated airframes, avionics and engines._x000D_
_x000D_
 _x000D_
_x000D_
(Source: ACARE Domain 1002)_x000D_
_x000D_
  - SUBDOMAINS:_x000D_
_x000D_
 _x000D_
_x000D_
1. Blended wing/body airliners_x000D_
_x000D_
2. Supersonic or hypersonic airliners_x000D_
_x000D_
3. Lighter-than-air (LTA) vehicles/airships - cargo transport, surveillance, communications, remote imaging_x000D_
_x000D_
4. Very large payload capacity aircraft - cargo transports, airliners, utilisation of Wing In Ground effect (WIG)_x000D_
_x000D_
5. Fully automated aircraft - 24 hour a day operation_x000D_
_x000D_
6. Tilt-rotor and other vertical take-off and landing (VTOL) configurations_x000D_
_x000D_
7. Hybrid configurations_x000D_
_x000D_
8. Dedicated freight aircraft operations using separate infrastructures from passenger airliners - inter-modality issues_x000D_
_x000D_
9. Interface with airport ground systems

Scenarios analysis - DEFINITION:_x000D_
_x000D_
 _x000D_
_x000D_
Scenarios provide a means for raising the awareness of decision-makers towards the implications of plausible possible futures, Scenario-based planning tells a series of consistent stories about the future outcome of existing paradigms and trends._x000D_
_x000D_
 _x000D_
_x000D_
(Source: ACARE Domain 1001)_x000D_
_x000D_
  - SUBDOMAINS:_x000D_
_x000D_
 _x000D_
_x000D_
1. Identification of drivers of change (technological, demographic, cultural, political)_x000D_
_x000D_
2. Elaboration of basic trends (societal, cultural)_x000D_
_x000D_
3. Key uncertainties - derivation, analysis_x000D_
_x000D_
4. Scenario generation_x000D_
_x000D_
5. Scenario analysis_x000D_
_x000D_
6. Drivers for future research - agenda setting and evaluation

Innovative Concepts & Scenarios - DEFINITION:_x000D_
_x000D_
 _x000D_
_x000D_
It comprises any innovative scenario, configuration or aircraft system as well as breakthrough technologies in aviation._x000D_
_x000D_
 _x000D_
_x000D_
It also includes hybrid and electric power and propulsion systems as very promising ones for the future aircrafts._x000D_
_x000D_
 _x000D_
_x000D_
(Source: ACARE Area 10 and PAE own definition)_x000D_
_x000D_
  - SUBDOMAINS:_x000D_
_x000D_
 _x000D_
_x000D_
1-J-1. Scenarios analysis _x000D_
_x000D_
1-J-2. Unconventional configurations and new aircraft concepts _x000D_
_x000D_
1-J-3. Breakthrough technologies _x000D_
_x000D_
1-J-4. Hybrid & Electric Power and Propulsion_x000D_
_x000D_

Ground station technologies - DEFINITION:_x000D_
_x000D_
 _x000D_
_x000D_
It comprises all the specific technologies related with the development of ground stations for remote piloting and control individual or groups of UAVs flight._x000D_
_x000D_
 _x000D_
_x000D_
(Source: PAE own definition based on diverse sources)_x000D_
_x000D_
  -

UAV-ATM technologies - DEFINITION:_x000D_
_x000D_
 _x000D_
_x000D_
It comprises all the specific technologies related with the air traffic control of remote piloted aerial systems and their integration into the overall ATM system._x000D_
_x000D_
 _x000D_
_x000D_
(Source: PAE own definition based on diverse sources)_x000D_
_x000D_
  -

Unmanned Aerial Systems Technologies - DEFINITION:_x000D_
_x000D_
 _x000D_
_x000D_
It comprises all the specific technologies related with the development of unmanned (without an onboard human pilot) aerial systems including any kind of vehicles, the necessary ground stations for remote piloting and control, the communications between the UAVs and with the ground station and the technologies related with the ATM for this type of aircrafts._x000D_
_x000D_
 _x000D_
_x000D_
(Source: PAE own definition based in diverse sources, including ACARE Domain 1003, subdomains 4, 5 and 6)_x000D_
_x000D_
  - SUBDOMAINS:_x000D_
_x000D_
 _x000D_
_x000D_
1-K-1. UAV technologies_x000D_
_x000D_
1-K-2. Ground station technologies_x000D_
_x000D_
1-K-3. Communication technologies_x000D_
_x000D_
1-K-4. UAV-ATM technologies_x000D_
_x000D_

Communication technologies - DEFINITION:_x000D_
_x000D_
 _x000D_
_x000D_
It comprises all the specific technologies related with the communication between UAVs flying together and with their corresponding ground stations._x000D_
_x000D_
 _x000D_
_x000D_
(Source: PAE own definition based on diverse sources)_x000D_
_x000D_
  -

UAV technologies - DEFINITION:_x000D_
_x000D_
 _x000D_
_x000D_
It comprises all the specific technologies related with the development of unmanned aerial systems including any kind of vehicles: fixed wing, helicopter type, multi-copter and any other kind of robotized aerial vehicle able to flight without an onboard human pilot._x000D_
_x000D_
 _x000D_
_x000D_
(Source: PAE own definition based in diverse sources + Subdomains 4, 5 and 6 of ACARE Domain 1003)_x000D_

 Launching  and recovering technologies_x000D_
 Remote control of aircraft, fully automated approach and landing_x000D_
 Unmanned aircraft (fixed wing)_x000D_
 Unmanned aircraft (helicopter type)_x000D_
 Unmanned aircraft (multirotor type)_x000D_
 Free flight control regime_x000D_
_x000D_
_x000D_
Formation flying technologies

Digital Industry -  Industry 4.0 - DEFINITION:_x000D_
_x000D_
 _x000D_
_x000D_
Following the definition from SMART (Eureka program on advanced manufacturing) a set of technologies are needed for developing the factories of the future, in particular for the aeronautical industry. All those technologies are included in this class except the ones related with Advanced Manufacturing and, in particular Additive Manufacturing, that is included into 1-B-4._x000D_
_x000D_
The grand challenge to be solved by these technologies is to significantly reduce recurrent costs and lead times in aeronautical industry._x000D_
_x000D_
 _x000D_
_x000D_
(Source: SMART Technology Roadmap for Aeronautical Industry) - SUBDOMAINS:_x000D_
_x000D_
 _x000D_
_x000D_
1-L-1. Intelligent and Adaptive Manufacturing Systems _x000D_
_x000D_
1-L-2. Digital, Virtual and Efficient Companies _x000D_
_x000D_
1-L-3. Person-Machine Collaboration _x000D_
_x000D_
1-L-4. Sustainable Manufacturing _x000D_
_x000D_
1-L-5. Customer-based Manufacturing _x000D_
_x000D_

Digital, Virtual and Efficient Companies  - DEFINITION:_x000D_
_x000D_
 _x000D_
_x000D_
Includes Factory design, data collection and management, operation and planning, from real time to long term optimization approaches_x000D_
_x000D_
 _x000D_
_x000D_
OBJECTIVE: _x000D_
_x000D_
 _x000D_
_x000D_
Develop complete toolbox and concepts for a digital factory with and integrated end-to-end approach._x000D_
_x000D_
 _x000D_
_x000D_
(Source: SMART report, paragraph 4.1.5)_x000D_
_x000D_
  - TECHNOLOGIES:_x000D_
_x000D_
 _x000D_
_x000D_
_x000D_
 Simulation techniques in manufacturing and assembly processes to increase ergonomics, first-time -right and production rates_x000D_
 Evolution of machinery, integrating operation simulations in design: new processes, new machine tools and developing software with new features_x000D_
